zh.Acton and Old Felton, Northumberland, En: 英國諾圖波藍德阿克頓 安德 笛卡爾 費爾頓
zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Sturmyn於1380年出生在英國時，她的父親William Sturmyn30歲，母親Joane31歲。她於1400年與Richard Acton在英國諾圖波藍德結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世在她的家鄉。

zh-hans.Acton and Old Felton, Northumberland, En: 英国諾圖波藍德阿克頓 安德 笛卡爾 費爾頓
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Sturmyn于1380年出生在英国时，她的父亲William Sturmyn30岁，母亲Joane31岁。她于1400年与Richard Acton在英国諾圖波藍德结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世在她的家乡。

nameMeanings.name: Sturmyn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from an unrecorded Middle English personal name Sturmin, probably a diminutive in -in of the Middle English personal name Sturmi, from Middle English stormi ‘stormy’ (Old French estormi), denoting a stormy temperament. Compare Storm .

English: variant of Stearman 1 in which the main vowel of Middle English ster(e)man, stir(e)man has shortened, giving rise in the 17th century to the modern pronunciation variously spelled -er-, -ir-, and -ur-. In some cases the name may arise from the Middle English personal name Stireman, Stereman (Old Norse Stȳreman, originally a nickname from the Old Danish word for a helmsman or skipper); see Stearman 1.
Americanized form of North German Sturmann or Stuhrmann, and of Dutch Stuurman: occupational name for a helmsman, from Middle Low German stūren ‘to steer’ + man ‘man’.

variants.name: Storm
meanings: English, North German, Dutch, and Scandinavian: nickname for a man of blustery or stormy temperament, from Middle English, Middle Low German storm, Old Norse stormr ‘storm’.
Flemish and Dutch: occupational name for someone who sounded the alarm-bell, warning people for a coming storm, Middle Dutch storm.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Sturmyn was born in 1380, in England, her father, William Sturmyn, was 30 and her mother, Joane, was 31. She married Richard Acton in 1400, in Northumberland, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died in her hometown.

fr.Acton and Old Felton, Northumberland, En: Acton and Old Felton, Northumberland, Angleterre
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Sturmyn est née en 1380, à Angleterre, son père, William Sturmyn, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Joane, avait 31 ans. Elle a épousé Richard Acton en 1400, à Northumberland, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée dans sa ville natale.

de.Acton and Old Felton, Northumberland, En: Acton and Old Felton, Northumberland, England
de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth Sturmyn 1380 in England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William Sturmyn 30 und ihre Mutter Joane 31. Sie hat 1400 in Northumberland, England Richard Acton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Sturmyn è nata il nel 1380 nel England, sua padre, William Sturmyn, aveva 30 anni e sua madre, Joane, aveva 31 anni. Lei sposato Richard Acton nel 1400, nel Northumberland, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel sua cittadina.

ja.Acton and Old Felton, Northumberland, En: イギリスノースウンバーランドアクトン アンド オルド フエルトン
ja.Northumberland, England: イギリスノースウンバーランド
ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Sturmynが1380年にイギリスで生まれた時，父William Sturmynは30歳，母Joaneは31歳であった。1400年にイギリスノースウンバーランドでRichard Actonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は故郷で死去した。

ko.Acton and Old Felton, Northumberland, En: 영국 노르스벌랜드 악턴 앤드 드 프턴
ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Sturmyn(이)가 1380년 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Sturmyn(은)는 30세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joane(은)는 31세였다. 그녀는 1400년 영국 노르스벌랜드에서 Richard Acton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 그녀의 고향에서 사망했다.

ru.Acton and Old Felton, Northumberland, En: актон анд олд фелтон, нортумбирланд, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Sturmyn родилась в 1380 г. в местности Англия, её отцу по имени William Sturmyn было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Joane, 31. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Richard Acton в 1400 г. в местности нортумбирланд, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла в её родном городе.
